title: What is CodeRef
summary: CodeRef is a multi-agent software ecosystem — a set of AI agents, each owning a domain, that coordinate through files and standards rather than a central app. This site is the front door: arrive here, understand the system, branch into any part.

title: The orchestrator
summary: ASSISTANT is the ecosystem coordinator — the lead agent that drives the whole system. The role is narrower and sharper than "knows everything": it owns the coordination, not the domains' work.

title: The org chart
summary: The ecosystem has 63 registered domains (35 active), grouped by project. The shape is fractal — ASSISTANT coordinates the ecosystem the way a project coordinator drives its own project, one level down.

title: How to navigate
summary: This site is the front door to CodeRef — the place to arrive and orient. The docs are the map; the repo is the territory.

headings.text: The site grows with the ecosystem

title: What is CODEREF-CORE
summary: CODEREF-CORE is the standalone code-intelligence engine of the ecosystem — a scanner + retrieval core delivered as a CLI. It reads a codebase, produces the .coderef/ artifact set, and builds semantic search over the same code. Everything else consumes its artifacts rather than re-scanning.

title: Scanning and artifacts
summary: How a project gets its .coderef/ artifacts — bootstrap with coderef-init, generate with populate-coderef, read quickly with coderef-scan, repair with coderef-pipeline, and keep current with coderef-watch.

title: Semantic search (the rag family)
summary: The rag skills build a vector index over a project's code and answer natural-language questions against it — rag-index to build, rag-search to query, rag-status to inspect, and coderef-rag-server as the REST surface other tools call.

title: Contract checks and foundation docs
summary: Two things the .coderef/ artifacts make possible — catching frontend/backend API-contract drift with the scan-frontend-calls + validate-routes pair, and generating living foundation docs from the scanned data.

title: What a skill is
summary: A skill is a map and a guide, not a script — it tells an agent what a workflow is for, when to reach for it, and the contract to honor. The corpus holds 168 skills across 17 purpose categories, and the category list itself is schema-canonical.

title: Anatomy of a skill package
summary: Every skill is a directory at SKILLS/{CATEGORY}/{skill-name}/ with a standard shape — SKILL.md as the agent-facing contract, skill.json as the machine manifest, a runner when there is deterministic work, and support folders only when earned.

title: Finding and creating skills
summary: Two surfaces find a skill — the generated SKILL-INDEX table and the /which-skill ranked search. One surface creates them — /new-coderef-skill. The standing rule: never freehand a workflow a canonical skill already owns.

title: Canonical vs wrapper
summary: A skill's real logic lives in exactly one place — the canonical package in the ASSISTANT repo. What's installed into each CLI surface is a thin wrapper that points back at it. Validators enforce the split.

title: Browse all skills
summary: Every skill in the ecosystem, generated from the live registry at build time — filter by name or summary, narrow by category, and drill into a per-skill reference page.

title: The daily session
summary: A multi-agent day runs inside a daily session — a dated directory every domain joins, where work moves by dispatch and idle agents stay responsive on a wake-poll loop. This is the skill-side of how the loop runs.

title: The session lifecycle
summary: A daily session is created once, joined independently by each domain, and swept closed at day's end. One subdirectory per domain means no file locking is needed.

title: The dispatch flow
summary: Dispatches are how work moves between domains — a sender files one, the receiver accepts and completes it through a state-machine runner, and the result is written back onto the same artifact.

title: The wake-poll loop
summary: Between tasks an agent is never "off" — it sleeps on a scheduled wake, polls its inbox, and always re-arms the next wake. A loop that forgets to re-arm dies silently.

title: How the day is coordinated
summary: ASSISTANT opens the session, every agent checks in and arms a wake-poll loop, work flows as dispatches (files in inboxes, polled — no server), blockers route back as andon pulls, and the day ends with a liveness-aware close. Coordination is a protocol made of files and a rhythm, not a runtime.

title: The registry-db model
summary: CodeRef tracks its work in a small SQLite database — the single source of truth. The JSON files under TRACKING/ that agents read every day are generated views of it. When a view and the DB disagree, the DB wins.

title: The lifecycle of tracked entities
summary: CodeRef tracks four entity kinds — stubs, workorders, dispatches, and tickets. They form a pipeline from "an idea worth remembering" to "work that shipped," plus a parallel lane for bugs.

headings.text: The shape of the whole flow

title: Views-vs-DB discipline
summary: Editing a generated JSON view by hand is editing a copy — the change vanishes on the next regeneration, or worse, desyncs the view from the DB. Every registry has a designated writer; you change state by running it.

title: The canonical registries
summary: The registries worth knowing by name — all under TRACKING/, the view-typed ones regenerated from the DB, never edited. One of them, agent-domains.json, is the source of truth for "who am I" across the whole multi-agent system.

title: Visualizations
summary: Interactive views of the ecosystem. First up — the typed skill-dependency graph, which renders depends_on and supersedes as visually distinct edges so the graph never flattens to an untyped link map.

title: Skill map
summary: Category-grouped index of all 170 skills.

title: /3d-generation
summary: Generate 3D STL models and 2D artifacts using the Atomic CAD Engine. Orchestrates Atoms (Level 1), Components (Level 2), Patterns (Level 3), and Assemblies (Level 4) within the stl-agent service.

title: /accept-session-dispatch
summary: Dispatch lifecycle ACCEPT->WORK->LOG->COMPLETE: atomic state transitions, an auto-complete sweeper, decision-routing + imperative-timing gates, and mid-flight blocker surfacing; worktree-aware on the main-anchored record. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /active-work
summary: Render a one-line-per-domain human-readable status table on demand from session events, dispatch JSONs, and the legacy active-work.archive.json fallback.

title: /add-to-glossary
summary: Add project-discovered terms to the central CodeRef learning glossary.

title: /advise
summary: Mid-workflow decision helper. User triggers when agent surfaces a question + options; emits a tight worth-knowing block + pick + optional alternative. Read-only.

title: /agent-polling-check
summary: Operator-visible polling-status diagnostic: broadcast presence-check to all active domains, wait buffered interval, render checkin/status table. Surfaces silent-active wake-loop failures.

title: /agent-pulse
summary: Poll authoritative agent state and render only deltas since last poll, including a WAITING ON YOU section derived from executor HANDOFF.json files.

title: /align-coderef-culture
summary: Mandatory session-start cultural alignment gate. Maps intended work to canonical tool/communication/closure/TRACKING obligations, validates semantic headers for SKILLS/ANALYSIS and SKILLS/CORE, and logs gaps to notes.md.

title: /align-session-standards
summary: Precise communication rules for multi-agent CodeRef sessions. ASSISTANT leads; agents coordinate through ASSISTANT; ASSISTANT surfaces to operator.

title: /align-to-standards
summary: Point an agent at the standards for the project it is in: resolve domain via the canonical resolver, then read (or discover + scaffold) a per-domain STANDARDS.md listing that project's standards docs. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /analyze-coderef-semantics
summary: Unified semantic metadata discovery and analysis — query foundation-docs exports, validate provenance, index semantic headers, filter by layer/capability/constraint, inspect metadata

title: /analyze-data-source-use-cases
summary: Analyze a data source report against the assistant ecosystem and generate integration use cases. Verb-first companion to /data-source-report.

title: /andon-pull
summary: Agent-initiated halt-the-line andon-cord pull. Writes ANDON-PULLED-{ts}.json sentinel + fires blocker-priority dispatch to ASSISTANT + emits andon_pulled session event. Rate-limited 1 per agent per 600s. Active counterpart to passive andon-pull.

title: /archive-dispatch
summary: Archive a single terminal-state dispatch file from its session folder into the canonical archive root with manifest. Updates TRACKING/dispatches.json if present. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.

title: /archive-reconcile
summary: Self-heal registry-disk drift for stubs, workorders, and dispatches: flips registry rows whose archive_status is stale but archive-manifest.json exists on disk, and cleans WO orphan sources. Idempotent. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /archive-status
summary: Canonical read-only reporter for ARCHIVED-domain health. Computes ground-truth counts, drift indicators (D1-D9), and handoff-honor-rate. Bucketizes green/yellow/red. Read-only.

title: /archive-stub
summary: Archive a single terminal-state stub from TRACKING/stubs.json into the canonical archive root with snapshot + manifest, mutating the registry row in place. Idempotent; conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /archive-sweep
summary: Audit and optionally execute archive maintenance across all three entity kinds (workorder, stub, dispatch). Default dry-run scans archive roots and writes archive-health.json; --execute delegates per-entity to canonical archive-* skills.

title: /archive-working-dir
summary: Move a single stale coderef/working/<...> scratch dir into the canonical archive root with manifest. Stale = slug matches a terminal-status workorder/stub feature_name. No registry row to flip. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.

title: /archive-workorder
summary: Archive a single terminal-state workorder from its source project into the canonical archive root with manifest. Standalone surface and callable from /close-workorder Phase D/E. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.

title: /archived-terminal-action-watcher
summary: Poll today's HANDOFF.json files for close- and cancel-eligible WOs; atomically claim; spawn ephemeral workers via /spawn-agent.

title: /assistant-modes
summary: ASSISTANT-specific output-mode discipline. 4 modes (operator-direct, audit-report, status-broadcast, triage) each with a fixed shape contract. Catches output-too-dense + multi-thread + missing-next-action drift. Phase 1 declarative; Phase 2 runtime gate deferred.

title: /audit-surfaces
summary: Audit registered projects in TRACKING/ and identify APIs/services that exist in code but aren't registered in surfaces.json. Uses hybrid detection (code scanning, manifests, documentation) to flag missing, orphaned, and drifted surfaces.

title: /auto-workorder
summary: Two-spawn autonomous workorder orchestrator (CREATE -> EXECUTE). --from-stub uses an existing stub; --auto-stub --goal mints one first via /stub --goal then chains. Step 2.5 gates read plan.workorder_hooks (--workorder-hooks=none|thorough override).

title: /autoresearch
summary: Phase 2 AutoResearch — run modify-verify-keep/discard experiments after goal.md exists (use create-autoresearch-loop first to scaffold goal + Verify). Commits each iteration; improves toward the...

title: /be-brief
summary: Guide agents to answer briefly by default while preserving necessary facts and actionability; plus tool-call output-channel discipline (KI-017: emit the call before any narration on multi-step turns).

title: /be-explicit
summary: Transform ambiguous instructions into explicit, structured action lists with ALL CAPS action terms

title: /be-natural
summary: Tone standard for /sessions chat — refuses dispatch-speak; per-session miss-counter; sibling to /be-brief + /be-explicit.

title: /cancel-workorder
summary: Cancel a never-executed workorder (orthogonal to /close-workorder, which requires shipped work): archive the dir with a CANCEL-MARKER, flip the registry row to cancelled, and dispose of the wo/* branch. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /check-agent-state
summary: Live wake-loop drift detector. Scans LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/{DOMAIN}/checkin.json + status.json, fires resurrection-dispatch when checkin age > 15min AND state idle/blocked. Sibling to /check-workorder-state; canonical-skill shape reuse. Idempotent via cooldown sidecar.

title: /check-chat-inbox
summary: Scan today's chat-state.json for chat messages addressed to the agent (in their #domain channel OR @mentioned). Returns chat_pending[] with in_reply_to_required when @mentioned. Sibling of /check-dispatch-inbox; the chat lane of the wake loop.

title: /check-dispatch-inbox
summary: Wake-poll inbox scan: pending -> auto-accept, plus andon-pull, stuck-accepted, self-heal, open-stubs backlog; terse-by-default + adaptive backoff on all-clear no_pending ticks (--verbose restores full payload); always re-arms the wake. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /check-workorder-state
summary: Live drift detector for in-flight workorders: compares registry/plan/communication/commits against expected invariants and reports anomalies, with an opt-in --detect-runaway flag for runaway-child surfacing. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /close-daily-session
summary: Close a daily session (v2 liveness-aware): refuse live sessions, --dry-run preview, --include-today override, --sweep-domains opt-in domain flip, --list-stale inspection, CODEREF_OPERATOR_TZ aware. Canonical inverse of /create-daily-session.

title: /close-workorder
summary: Close a completed workorder (audit, finalize, commit, push) via the unified flow, with /git-land merge-back enforcement for worktree wo/* branches; archiving is an explicit --archive second step. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /coderef-analyze
summary: Analyze an existing CodeRef scan by reading .coderef artifacts such as index.json, graph.json, context.json, and reports. Use when the user wants code inventory, dependency, complexity, or...

title: /coderef-extract-autoresearch
summary: RUN the Entity monorepo coderef-extract AutoResearch loop workflow (benchmarks, dependency order, goal.md paths). USE when the user asks to run autoresearch loops for the extractor, verify loops...

title: /coderef-fast-start
summary: Automatically launch all 14 CodeRef workday services (Ollama, Lloyd, Entity Dashboard, OPENCLAW, RAG, Monitoring API, ROUTER, plus PM2-managed monitors/webhooks/router/discord-bot listener).

title: /coderef-init
summary: Initialize canonical coderef/ and .coderef/ directory structures plus a starter .coderefignore in any project

title: /coderef-issue-investigator
summary: Investigates code issues across relevant files and returns a structured diagnostic report with evidence, priority, fixes, and validation steps.

title: /coderef-pipeline
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE coderef-pipeline CLI to coordinate multi-stage scan→populate→validate pipeline.

title: /coderef-rag-server
summary: Manage the RAG server lifecycle (start, query, index, status, reset). HTTP/REST surface for semantic code search.

title: /coderef-scan
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE coderef-scan CLI for element-level stats (no file generation).

title: /coderef-watch
summary: Long-running watcher over a project's .coderef/ directory and source tree; emits change notifications.

title: /collect-context
summary: Capture user-provided research material into CODEREF/RESEARCH/{PROJECT}/{topic-slug}/. Append-only. Creates or updates the matching entry in TRACKING/research.json.

title: /consolidate-ps-backlog
summary: Read-only PS team-at-a-glance dashboard — renders the live agent lineup (at-bat/on-deck) then the open backlog by domain then category, to the terminal. Touches nothing; HTML render is SURFACES-HTML's lane (hand-off stubbed).

title: /coordinate-backlog
summary: Project-agnostic coordinator lineup that re-derives the baseball-shape tiers (At Bat/On Deck/In the Hole/Idle) from GIT truth + the live registry, never from stale status.json text; flags projection-drift. Read-only GATHER+DERIVE+print (B1.0).

title: /create-agent-session
summary: Orchestrate agent-to-agent communication sessions outside of workorders using the agent communication bus architecture.

title: /create-autoresearch-loop
summary: Phase 1 AutoResearch — SCAFFOLD goal.md + loop folder from user context (YAML frontmatter, Scope/Verify, notes). Pair with SHARED autoresearch / run-autoresearch for commit-driven experiments. USE...

title: /create-capability
summary: Register or update a capability in a project context and regenerate the centralized aggregator.

title: /create-daily-session
summary: Create a daily session for multiagent orchestration; mirrors it into the active-sessions.json manifest so the resolver finds it. --new mints a uuid-keyed session for concurrent same-day streams (date default unchanged).

title: /create-roadmap
summary: Mint roadmap.md + sidecar roadmap.json for a multi-phase initiative, or a standing per-domain roadmap via --type=domain (DOMAIN-ROADMAP-STANDARD). History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /create-txen-recipe
summary: Create TxEN recipe definitions with DESIGN standards token system compliance

title: /create-workorder
summary: Unified workorder entry: classify plan-type, run discovery, author context/analysis/plan/communication, and register in TRACKING. Supports --from-stub and --phased-stubs rolling-plans. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /data-source-report
summary: Analyze data sources in a project and generate structured reports on type, format, quality, and usage

title: /design-component
summary: Generate reusable components (primitives, components, recipes, templates) using universal design flow

title: /discord-ingestion
summary: Discord gateway listener accepting every inbound message form in a nominated ingest channel; persists each drop to SURFACES/.ingest/{date}/{message_id}/; admin-only, logged_only in v1.

title: /discover
summary: Structured 6-section discovery report. Quick/medium/thorough depth. Outputs to workorder/stub/working/stdout. Stub-hierarchy aware: domain topics surface open stubs, parent-stub targets surface open children.

title: /dispatch-session-request
summary: Dispatch a task to an agent domain (or a pinned DOMAIN:surface) through the active daily session. --staged parks it; --kind sets intent so only task dispatches carry the strict gate. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /document-analysis
summary: Analyze document-discovery output to measure documentation coverage, naming compliance, duplicate filename risk, and type distribution, then generate an actionable analysis report. Use when a user...

title: /document-audit
summary: Audit actual document content against project reality by reading discovery and analysis outputs, verifying references and freshness, classifying document health, and prescribing KEEP, UPDATE...

title: /document-discovery
summary: Inventory and classify a target project's Markdown documentation, then generate project-state-discovery JSON and Markdown outputs for the downstream document pipeline. Use when a user wants a...

title: /document-pipeline
summary: Orchestrate the shared document workflow for a target project by running document-discovery, document-analysis, document-audit, and document-rebuild through the rebuild-plan approval gate. Use...

title: /document-rebuild
summary: Turn document-audit prescriptions into a safe documentation rebuild plan, then execute approved archive, delete, merge, and update actions only after explicit user approval. Use when a user wants...

title: /dry-run
summary: Simulate execution of instructions without performing real actions. Preview workflows, file diffs, and edge cases before committing to changes.

title: /execute-workorder
summary: Autonomously execute a workorder from plan.json to completion: implement tasks phase-by-phase, honor hard stops, commit-as-you-go on the wo/* branch, then run the canonical close-out. Fail-closed plan-schema gate refuses drifted plans. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /fill-ladder
summary: One-command lineup fill: discover backlog (git truth + registry + open domain-roadmap goal-leaves) -> compute ranked candidates -> SELF-PICK at-bat + on-deck/in-the-hole; picked roadmap leaves auto-mint their stub (decompose-on-pick). History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /full-workorder-execution
summary: Disciplined wrapper over /auto-workorder for risky/load-bearing WOs: audits at each phase boundary, with gates that read plan.workorder_hooks instead of hardcoded /discover calls. Use when audit-pass-per-boundary pays for itself. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /gather-jfk-sources
summary: JFK source registry (discover/refresh/snowball) + --roster {org} person+papers + --timeline {thread} dated chronology into the incident artifact (held-cite-or-drop; excluded compilations are SEED-only). Step 4.7 ACQUIRE: key-gated NARA RESOLVE+FETCH. Lands in PS-ARCHIVE.

title: /gather-subject-sources
summary: Template for building a flat CSV source registry for any subject domain. Parameterize DOMAIN, LAYER_SEQUENCE, and SOURCE_CLASS_ENUM to instantiate.

title: /generate-foundation-docs
summary: Generate living foundation docs from .coderef data: 4 new (INDEX, EXPORTS, HOTSPOTS, RELATIONSHIPS) + enhance 4 existing with UUID anchors. Supports --validate-only fast path.

title: /generate-project-context
summary: Generate or refresh a line-budgeted CONTEXT.md and deliver thin Trinity pointers (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md), updating tracking/project-context.json. Create, refresh, and legacy-migrate modes. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /generate-quickstart
summary: Discover project services, ports, dependencies, and entry points then generate a populated {PROJECT}-quickstart.md at the project root.

title: /generate-resource-sheet
summary: Generate CodeRef resource sheets from current project source, .coderef outputs, foundation docs, and resource-sheet conventions.

title: /generate-session-report
summary: Generate comprehensive terminal session status report - reads active session and all 11 domain status files

title: /git-commit
summary: The one commit verb (main-only, by-path): /git-commit "msg" commits per GIT-STANDARD, safe default stages only YOUR TRACKED files — dynamically excludes DB-derived views + never auto-stages untracked files (TKT-0G25F5); --done pushes main + verifies.

title: /git-doctor
summary: Audit a repo for stranded branches, merged-undeleted wo/* branches (work landed, delete dropped), dirty/wrong-branch worktrees, registry-vs-physical + graph-vs-tree invariants; emit PASS/WARN. --fix prunes safe strays/locks + git branch -d merged-undeleted branches.

title: /git-land
summary: Push main to origin and VERIFY origin holds your HEAD; HALT loud if not. Main-only model: no wo/* branches to merge or delete — the branch-land machinery is retired, leaving only the push-and-verify durability gate.

title: /git-rules
summary: Drop-in git skill for any terminal in any CodeRef repo: run.mjs orients + evaluates the lane matrix + fixes your position (--fix opens your own wo/* lane). Exit 0 = proceed; exit 2 = run the printed fix.

title: /git-stage
summary: Scoped git-add wrapper. Reads in-flight focus_paths[] and refuses bare 'git add .' / '-A' + paths outside scope. --no-coordination escape hatch for single-agent. Prevents multi-agent commit-interleave.

title: /im-fried
summary: One-line plain-English reminder of what you were just working on. Drops into chat for ADHD-shape memory relief.

title: /improve-skills
summary: Read-only synthesizer that walks the kaizen.md substrate across all SKILL.md files, clusters observations, and emits a PROPOSED-EDITS report. Hermes pattern — never mutates source artifacts.

title: /in-flight
summary: Live registry of which agent is currently working in which scope. Sidecar at LOGS/SESSIONS/{date}/in-flight.json. Subcommands: --register, --heartbeat, --clear, --list, --sweep. Atomic writes. Read by /im-fried, task-board, /stage-mine.

title: /join-chat-as-agent
summary: Spawn a Chrome MCP tab for an agent identity on the /sessions chat surface. Returns tabId for subsequent /send-chat --via-browser calls. Page auto-initializes identity from ?as=DOMAIN. Use ONCE per agent per session.

title: /join-daily-session
summary: Agent domains check in to a daily session and write checkin.json/status.json (concurrent, lockless), then run /check-dispatch-inbox inline and arm the wake-poll loop. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /kaizen-aggregate
summary: Reader/projection rebuilder for the continuous-improvement loop. Queries kaizen_entries DB; regenerates kaizen.md projections; surfaces missing-goal + dead-skill + top-category clusters.

title: /kaizen-improvements
summary: KAIZEN-family improvement-capture (renamed from /improvements). Logs findings to the canonical registry.db improvement_entries table; per-scope improvements.json files are projections regenerated from the DB (Option 2c, mirrors kaizen-log + kaizen.md sidecars).

title: /kaizen-log
summary: Writer half of the kaizen loop: appends a categorized observation to a skill's kaizen.md sidecar + registry.db, worktree-aware so the append commits with the work; bug-shaped notes surface in real time. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /kaizen-validator
summary: Env-gated validator for the kaizen contract. Checks kaizen.md sidecar presence + non-template ## Goal + SKILL.md universal-contract Kaizen Step block (post-P5). CODEREF_KAIZEN_GATE=1 to refuse.

title: /key-entity
summary: Canonical entity keyer: stamps a deterministic ps_uid onto person/place/org/artifact rows per the §3 disambiguator ladder, including the rung-3 RECORD-RELATIONSHIP anchor for witnesses and investigative-body members. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /list-mailboxes
summary: Cross-domain mailbox overview: enumerates domains with mailboxes and renders inbox/sent/drafts counts per domain. Pairs with /send-email + /read-mailbox. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /lock-in
summary: Lock the active agent onto ONE specific task. Refuses all other workstreams until /lock-in --unlock. Per-domain lock state at LOGS/SESSIONS/{session}/{DOMAIN}/lock-in.json. --whats-next introspects pending locked work.

title: /log-business-idea
summary: Research a business idea, analyze viability, and generate a standardized business idea package.

title: /log-session
summary: Generates a YAML session log capturing tools, files, docs, decisions, and outcomes, with mandatory closure_verification for doc-discovery, heartbeats, and cleanup. History in SKILL.md metadata.changelog.

title: /log-skill
summary: Append one compact skill-activity entry to the active daily session domain activity log.

title: /memory-rebuild
summary: Archive, scored-triage, and rebuild an agent's auto-memory so MEMORY.md loads in full and carries only durable facts; --prune is lighter periodic hygiene. Always writes the domain-from-cwd invariant. Use when MEMORY.md is over-limit, stale, or self-conflicting.

title: /new-app-idea
summary: Briefly log an app or dev idea as a single scannable markdown file under CODEREF/APPS/{slug}/idea.md. Capture-only — no web research.

title: /new-coderef-skill
summary: Create, update, archive, or supersede an ecosystem skill with paired SKILL.md + skill.json, thin wrappers (claude/codex/agy), and auto-rebuild of tracking/skills.json. Enforces signpost-shape on summary/description. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /nudge
summary: Wake-poll nudge for a stalled agent — fires a minimal instruction-pass dispatch when an agent's wake-poll appears dead.

title: /onboard-collection
summary: Onboard a JFK-corpus collection by deriving a facts-only, ps_uid-keyed sidecar artifact with a closed schema and structured provenance.

title: /onboard-entity
summary: Workflow verb that drives /key-entity end to end: read entity manifest/seed -> validate (refuse-to-mint) -> live-schema preflight -> begin/rollback rehearse -> commit -> report all-surfaces. Entity twin of /onboard-keying.

title: /onboard-gpt-agent
summary: Package a canonical CodeRef skill or generic workflow as a ChatGPT Custom GPT bundle (instructions txt, upload list, starters, capabilities).

title: /onboard-keying
summary: Step 1 of the Collection Keying Standard: inspect a read-only native index and write a validated keying.json, classifying the collection as flat/hierarchical/partial with deferred_grains. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /pick
summary: Mark a workorder as PENDING for autonomous execution by WorkorderWatcher. Idempotent write of status=PENDING + picked_at + picker to the workorder communication.json.

title: /plan-next-phase
summary: Author the next phase's tasks for a rolling-plan workorder from fresh phase-scoped discovery, then promote the linked stub deferred_phase->promoted via registry-db; worktree-aware on the WO plan.json. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /plan-tests-for-workorder
summary: Read a workorder's scope plus acceptance criteria and emit a recommended test matrix as JSON. Helps plan authors avoid ad-hoc test discipline.

title: /populate-coderef
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE populate-coderef CLI to generate/refresh .coderef/ artifacts for a target project.

title: /printable-process-report
summary: Generate print-ready plain-text process reports from workflows, notes, code findings, or examples.

title: /project-report
summary: Author a grounded, multi-pillar executive overview report for any project or domain.

title: /promote
summary: Mark a workorder PENDING for autonomous WorkorderWatcher dequeue. Idempotent write of status=PENDING + picked_at + picker to communication.json. Supersedes /pick.

title: /promote-recipe-to-design
summary: Promote TxEN recipes into @entity/design with DESIGN standards enforcement and testing matrix validation

title: /promote-stub
summary: Route a stub to its category-appropriate next-step command per category-rules.json in one move.

title: /promote-to-at-bat
summary: Promote an artifact into the operator's AT-BAT slot on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; refuses freeform. Writes task-board-promotions.json AND calls /lock-in (at-bat IS the operator's lock-in).

title: /promote-to-in-the-hole
summary: Promote an artifact (skill / WO / STUB / DISPATCH) into the operator's IN-THE-HOLE slot (third, after at-bat and on-deck) on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; persists to LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/task-board-promotions.json.

title: /promote-to-on-deck
summary: Promote an artifact (skill / WO / STUB / DISPATCH) into the operator's ON-DECK slot on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; refuses freeform. Writes to LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/task-board-promotions.json so the slot persists across re-renders.

title: /ps-db-manager
summary: Storage/schema authority for Primary Sources DB — migrations, catalog, generated types, live verification. Sibling to ps-scanner and ps-seeder.

title: /ps-scanner
summary: Scanner authority for Primary Sources — pipeline, OCR modes, 19-cluster/45-knob engine_tuning, artifacts, Supabase linker, reviewer UUID roundtrip. Sibling to ps-db-manager and ps-seeder.

title: /ps-seeder
summary: Run-and-report + agent-driven reviewer for Primary Sources. Surfaces feedback, suggests Tier-0 knob changes (19/45 surface), self-reviews via Class A-F rubric. Sibling to ps-db-manager and ps-scanner.

title: /quality-diagrams-analysis
summary: Analyze CodeRef quality.json and diagrams/ artifacts; extract normalized metrics and trend deltas across runs.

title: /rag-index
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-index CLI to build semantic search index with vector embeddings.

title: /rag-search
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-search CLI for natural-language semantic code search.

title: /rag-status
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-status CLI to inspect RAG index metadata (read-only).

title: /read-mailbox
summary: Read a domain's mailbox folder (inbox/sent/drafts) and render messages as a table; --json mode for machine consumption, --thread for conversation filtering. Pairs with /send-email + /list-mailboxes. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /register-domain
summary: Register a new agent domain atomically across agent-domains.json + seed-domains.md with resolver-glob validation; --update edits an existing row's non-identity fields (hosts_under/glob/etc) via registry_db. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /register-project-tracking
summary: Register the current project in CodeRef TRACKING via the writer-adapter. Prefers .coderef and foundation-docs as evidence; falls back to repo inspection.

title: /registry-db
summary: TRACKING registry.db library wrapping the SQLite-backed canonical registry. Insert with collision detection, query by legacy_id or ULID, hot-cold flip, retention sweep. Used by migration scripts and writer skills.

title: /registry-sweep
summary: Automated hygiene tool for the CodeRef tracking registries. Scans terminal-state items, proposes mutations for user approval, enforces schema compliance.

title: /review-workorder
summary: Audit and review a workorder for quality and compliance, including a git-doctor stranded-branch subcheck that flags unmerged WO branches before close. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /route-validation-analysis
summary: Analyze validate-routes output: coverage %, ranked route collisions, per-method breakdown.

title: /runtime-state
summary: Activity-rollup companion to /session-status. Aggregates unified-events.jsonl across all checked-in domains into per-domain ran/shipped/stuck/errored rollup over a time window.

title: /save-user-report
summary: Stash a scannable domain-specific report into the current domain's USER/ directory. User supplies topic and length; agent fills a 5-section template.

title: /scan-frontend-calls
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE scan-frontend-calls CLI to detect fetch/axios/request patterns.

title: /schedule-agent-wake
summary: Wrap ScheduleWakeup with arg validation, [60,3600] clamp, presets, --cancel sentinel. Step 4.5 passive-andon-pull gate fires a blocker dispatch to ASSISTANT BEFORE wake when the agent is waiting on user/agent/assignment.

title: /schedule-cron-job
summary: Schedule a cron job from an audit, procedure, or workflow file. Generates workorder stub+plan+communication and registers in Automation engine schedules.json.

title: /search-jfk-records
summary: Search the keyed JFK corpus (WC/DPD/NARA) — probe coverage, retrieve via the keyed-entity join + full-text, return sectioned ps_uid-anchored cited snippets. Use when an operator wants page-level quotable evidence about a person/topic/event already on the spine.

title: /send-chat
summary: Send a chat message from an agent into the /sessions chat surface. Direct POST to write-helper (default) OR via Chrome MCP composer (--via-browser). Tone-checked at daemon boundary; refused with 422 if dispatch-speak detected.

title: /send-email
summary: Send an internal agent-to-agent email: writes the envelope to the sender's sent/ + each recipient's inbox/, with cc + threading via --in-reply-to. Internal-only; --external rejected pending Phase 2. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /service-onboarding
summary: Scaffold a new backend processing service into Entity with tenant manifests, worker/engine directories, and Next.js API proxy

title: /session-status
summary: Canonical session aggregator. Reads active-work + per-domain status/checkin/dispatch + recent git log; emits ONE consistent status block.

title: /site-composition
summary: Analyze existing sites or compose new ones against composition standards. Generates scannable markdown maps showing shells, sections, recipes, templates, components, data flows, state schemas, and validation rules.

title: /spawn-agent
summary: Bare wrapper around Lloyd's spawn: resolve --<modelname> to provider/endpoint, call Lloyd, return PID+log or text. Terminal flavors support --continue (worktree-aware) + conventional tab colors per domain group (full table in references/DOMAIN-TAB-COLORS.md).

title: /spawn-fresh-coderef
summary: Spawn a headless agent to refresh a domain's CodeRef index and foundation documentation with semantic header generation (@layer, @capability, @constraint tags enabled by default).

title: /stage-mine
summary: Scoped git-add wrapper. Reads in-flight focus_paths[] and refuses bare `git add .` / `-A` + paths outside scope. --no-coordination escape hatch for single-agent. Prevents multi-agent commit-interleave.

title: /start-here
summary: Compose START-HERE.md + RESUME-PROMPT.md living session-state snapshot at ASSISTANT root; scans 6 sources; optional HTML render with graceful fallback.

title: /start-session-tracking
summary: Restart all session tracking services on demand - webhook monitor, baseline snapshots, attendance tracking

title: /stub
summary: Capture quick ideas as stubs; writes directly to registry.db via commit_and_export with zero dispatches, plus a --goal non-interactive mode for zero-prompt mint by /auto-workorder --auto-stub. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /surfaces-html-architect
summary: High-level architectural lead for SURFACES-HTML. Owns page composition, site-wide nav inventory, cross-component coordination seams. Delegates component authoring to /surfaces-html-designer.

title: /surfaces-html-designer
summary: Standards-aware authoring helper for SURFACES-HTML components. Knows tokens, BEM, one-way dependency, event bus, doc-code mirror.

title: /surfaces-html-output
summary: Compose HTML renders from agent artifacts via SURFACES-HTML components and emit a URL; --markdown renders a .md file to a self-contained, file://-openable, mobile-responsive page. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /surfaces-html-session
summary: Render the daily-session aggregate as a living HTML dashboard. Aggregates per-domain checkin/status/dispatch/activity-log/mapping-table artifacts into a single bento-grid surface. Session-keyed URL; subsequent invocations re-snapshot state.

title: /sync-llm-models
summary: Sync TRACKING/llm-models.json with Ollama /api/tags. Upsert keyed by sha256 digest; refresh API-owned fields; preserve human-owned fields (role, context_window, capabilities, notes). Tag aliasing merged into tags[].

title: /tenant-onboarding
summary: Orchestrate tenant onboarding with DESIGN standards token system and branding compliance

title: /ticket
summary: File a bug as a structured TKT-XXXXXX ticket: writes registry.db (tickets), auto-mints a linked category=fix stub that feeds /create-workorder --from-stub, dedups at entry. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /tracking
summary: Schema-aware query tool for the CodeRef tracking directory + improvement logs. Default returns an attention-first roll-up; rich token grammar filters by entity type, scope, id, status, severity, stale/recent/all. v3.x loads schemas at invocation.

title: /tracking-validator
summary: Validate tracking/ registry files via a schema pass, a dereference pass, and a terminal-state diff pass; --scope narrows it to one project's rows for a scoped close. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /txen-page-draft-workflow
summary: Create valid TxEN page-drafts with DESIGN standards token system compliance and Phase 1 header alignment rules

title: /update-discord-status
summary: Orchestrate Discord webhook status updates with 5-phase execution, data gathering, formatting, and delivery verification.

title: /update-ecosystem-index
summary: Refresh master ecosystem documentation index from TRACKING/ registries

title: /update-roadmap
summary: In-flight maintenance of an existing roadmap (atomic .md + .json rewrite); domain roadmaps get --link-stub/--link-wo/--goal-status/--rerender, auto-detected. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /update-session-status
summary: Self-report current work state in the active daily session by updating status.json with state, task, and timestamp.

title: /update-tracking
summary: Register or update an item in the centralized TRACKING/ directory. Called by creation workflows (/create-workorder, /create-autoresearch-loop, /stub) to ensure every new item has...

title: /validate-coderef-culture
summary: Validator companion to align-coderef-culture. Audits session artifacts against 13 locked checks and emits PASS/WARNING/FAIL verdict. Used by /close-workorder PHASE A AUDIT.

title: /validate-ecosystem-sync
summary: Pre-flight ecosystem health check. Verifies Lloyd API connectivity (port 52847) and CodeRef Core index drift (.coderef/reports/drift.json). Run at workorder execution start to catch infra-level desync before work begins.

title: /validate-primary-sources-data
summary: Validate field-shape parity across CSV/JSON/DB for person/place/organization entity surfaces in primary-sources

title: /validate-routes
summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE validate-routes CLI to cross-check frontend API calls vs backend routes.

title: /validate-skill-enforcement-coverage
summary: Runtime validator for the SKILL-CONTRACTS.md trigger-phrase taxonomy: scans SKILL.md for enforceable-contract phrasing and requires runtime-gate evidence OR an agent_honored exemption; --report=dashboard breaks down exempt reasons. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /what-shipped
summary: Canonical post-ship summary: reads a git commit (default HEAD) and emits Markdown to stdout + a JSON payload to stderr, detecting per-package version bumps via skill.json diff. Auto-invoked at /close-workorder. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

title: /wheres-it-at
summary: Find recently modified Markdown or project files across active tracked projects with a packaged HTML report script.

title: /which-skill
summary: Grep-rank lookup helper over SKILLS/STANDARDS/SKILL-INDEX.md. Pass a topic; returns top-N ranked canonical skill names with category, slash command, one-line summary. Use BEFORE freehanding a skill name.

title: /workorder-follow-up
summary: Close-time follow-up capture for /close-workorder Step 16: scrapes 7 sources (5 in-WO fields + unified-events observations + git commit bodies) for follow-up mentions and writes confirmed entries as candidate stubs for operator triage. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
